title: Module 1: Governance & Compliance Audit Schema
description: Machine-readable audit framework for evaluating AI governance in Accounting Information Systems

evaluation_criteria.description: Documented AI governance framework with board approval
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review AI policies, RACI charts, ethics committee minutes, SOX 404 control documentation
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Board-approved AI policy exists, governance committee meets quarterly, RACI matrix covers all AI systems

evidence_required.approval_standard: Board or equivalent governance body signature within 12 months

partial_scores.condition: Draft policy exists but not finalized
No policy but governance structure partially documented

severity_mapping.justification: Without documented AI governance policy, no controls exist to prevent/detect material misstatements from AI errors

evaluation_criteria.description: ISO 42001-compliant risk management throughout AI lifecycle
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review risk assessment procedures, risk register, risk treatment plans
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Risk management process documented, risk register maintained, risk assessments conducted before AI deployment

severity_mapping.justification: Inadequate risk management increases likelihood of undetected AI-related financial risks

regulatory_mapping.nist_govern: MAP-1.1, MAP-1.2, MAP-1.3 not adequately addressed

evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review control matrices, control testing documentation, management assessment
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Control matrices document AI controls, testing completed for all key controls, deficiencies remediated

evidence_required.sample_size_standard: Minimum 25 items per manual control, 100% for automated controls
evidence_required.testing_frequency: Quarterly for key controls, annually for others

scoring.calculation: Controls documented and tested / Total required controls

severity_mapping.justification: Inadequate control documentation prevents management from assessing ICFR effectiveness per SOX 404

evaluation_criteria.description: Maintained inventory of AI systems in AIS per NIST AI RMF GOVERN 1.6
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review AI system inventory, verify completeness, check update frequency
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Inventory includes all AI systems, updated quarterly, includes risk classification

severity_mapping.justification: Incomplete inventory prevents comprehensive risk assessment and monitoring

evaluation_criteria.description: NIST AI RMF GOVERN 2.2-compliant AI risk management training
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review training materials, attendance records, competency assessments
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Training program exists, key personnel trained annually, competency assessments passed

evidence_required.coverage_requirement: 100% of AI system owners and 80% of finance team

severity_mapping.justification: Insufficient training increases risk of control failures but unlikely to cause material misstatement alone

finding_format.sox_classification: enum [Pass, Control Deficiency, Significant Deficiency, Material Weakness]

interdependency_triggers.trigger_condition: G1.1 score < 0.5 (Missing or inadequate AI governance policy)
interdependency_triggers.action: Escalate fairness testing from 10% sampling to 50%+ comprehensive testing
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Governance gap indicates higher bias risk; compensate with deeper technical testing

interdependency_triggers.action: Increase scrutiny of audit trail documentation; flag potential PCAOB AS 1105 scope limitation
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Poor control documentation often correlates with poor explainability documentation
